tiktok.caption: Half of Americans claim Social Security at 62 — and that one decision costs them $182,000. Here's the math nobody runs before they claim.

youtube_short.title: Claiming Social Security at 62 Is a $182K Mistake
title_alternatives: The Social Security Timing Math Nobody Does Before Claiming
Wait to Claim Social Security — Here's What $182,000 Looks Like
youtube_short.description: Half of Americans claim Social Security the moment they turn 62 — but the math proves it's one of the most expensive retirement decisions you can make. Claim at 62 and you get around $1,400 a month. Wait until 70 and that jumps to $2,480 — a permanent 77% pay raise for life. The break-even point hits around age 79, and the average 62-year-old today is expected to live to 84. Run the numbers all the way to 85 and early claimers leave $182,000 on the table. If your health is decent, delay as long as you can — ideally to 70. It's the closest thing to free money your retirement will ever see. Follow for more wealth math. #socialsecurity #retirement #personalfinance #retirementplanning #wealthbuilding

hook_line: Claiming Social Security at 62 costs you $182,000
